column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en

BG 10.1#श्रीभगवानुवाच
भूय एव महाबाहो शृणु मे परमं वच:
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
bhūya eva mahā-bāho śhṛiṇu me paramaṁ vachaḥ
yatte ’haṁ prīyamāṇāya vakṣhyāmi hita-kāmyayā#The Lord said: Listen again to My divine teachings, O mighty armed one. Desiring your welfare because you are My beloved friend, I shall reveal them to you.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Lord said

BG 10.2#न मे विदु: सुरगणा: प्रभवं न महर्षय:\nअहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वश:#na me viduḥ sura-gaṇāḥ prabhavaṁ na maharṣhayaḥ\naham ādir hi devānāṁ maharṣhīṇāṁ cha sarvaśhaḥ#Neither celestial gods nor the great sages know of My origin. I am the source from which the gods and great seers come.#na—neither

BG 10.3#यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम्\nअसम्मूढ: स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापै: प्रमुच्यते#yo māmajam anādiṁ cha vetti loka-maheśhvaram\nasammūḍhaḥ sa martyeṣhu sarva-pāpaiḥ pramuchyate#Those who know Me as unborn and beginningless, and as the Supreme Lord of the universe, they among mortals are free from illusion and released from all evils.#yaḥ—who

BG 10.4#बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोह: क्षमा सत्यं दम: शम:
सुखं दु:खं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयश:
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधा:#buddhir jñānam asammohaḥ kṣhamā satyaṁ damaḥ śhamaḥ
sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ bhavo ’bhāvo bhayaṁ chābhayameva cha
ahinsā samatā tuṣhṭis tapo dānaṁ yaśho ’yaśhaḥ
bhavanti bhāvā bhūtānāṁ matta eva pṛithag-vidhāḥ#BG 10.4-5:#buddhiḥ—intellect

BG 10.5#बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोह: क्षमा सत्यं दम: शम:
सुखं दु:खं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयश:
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधा:#buddhir jñānam asammohaḥ kṣhamā satyaṁ damaḥ śhamaḥ
sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ bhavo ’bhāvo bhayaṁ chābhayameva cha
ahinsā samatā tuṣhṭis tapo dānaṁ yaśho ’yaśhaḥ
bhavanti bhāvā bhūtānāṁ matta eva pṛithag-vidhāḥ#BG 10.4-5:#buddhiḥ—intellect

BG 10.6#महर्षय: सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा\nमद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमा: प्रजा:#maharṣhayaḥ sapta pūrve chatvāro manavas tathā\nmad-bhāvā mānasā jātā yeṣhāṁ loka imāḥ prajāḥ#The seven great Sages, the four great Saints before them, and the fourteen Manus, are all born from My mind. From them, all the people in the world have descended.#mahā-ṛiṣhayaḥ—the great Sages

BG 10.7#एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वत:\nसोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशय:#etāṁ vibhūtiṁ yogaṁ cha mama yo vetti tattvataḥ\nso ’vikampena yogena yujyate nātra sanśhayaḥ#Those who know in truth My glories and divine powers become united with Me through unwavering Bhakti Yog. Of this there is no doubt.#etām—these

BG 10.8#अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्त: सर्वं प्रवर्तते\nइति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विता:#ahaṁ sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvaṁ pravartate\niti matvā bhajante māṁ budhā bhāva-samanvitāḥ#I am the origin of all creation. Everything proceeds from Me. The wise who know this perfectly worship Me with great faith and devotion.#aham—I
column12: bhāva-samanvitāḥ—endowed with great faith and devotion

column1: BG 10.9#मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्त: परस्परम्\nकथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च#mach-chittā mad-gata-prāṇā bodhayantaḥ parasparam\nkathayantaśh cha māṁ nityaṁ tuṣhyanti cha ramanti cha#With their minds fixed on Me and their lives surrendered to Me, My devotees remain ever content in Me. They derive great satisfaction and bliss in enlightening one another about Me and in conversing about My glories.#mat-chittāḥ—those with minds fixed on me
column2: mat-gata-prāṇāḥ—those who have surrendered their lives to me

column1: BG 10.10#तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम्\nददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते#teṣhāṁ satata-yuktānāṁ bhajatāṁ prīti-pūrvakam\ndadāmi buddhi-yogaṁ taṁ yena mām upayānti te#To those whose minds are always united with Me in loving devotion, I give the divine knowledge by which they can attain Me.#teṣhām—to them

BG 10.11#तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तम:\nनाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता#teṣhām evānukampārtham aham ajñāna-jaṁ tamaḥ\nnāśhayāmyātma-bhāva-stho jñāna-dīpena bhāsvatā#Out of compassion for them, I, who dwell within their hearts, destroy the darkness born of ignorance, with the luminous lamp of knowledge.#teṣhām—for them

BG 10.12#अर्जुन उवाच
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान्
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम्
आहुस्त्वामृषय: सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा
असितो देवलो व्यास: स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे#arjuna uvācha
paraṁ brahma paraṁ dhāma pavitraṁ paramaṁ bhavān
puruṣhaṁ śhāśhvataṁ divyam ādi-devam ajaṁ vibhum
āhus tvām ṛiṣhayaḥ sarve devarṣhir nāradas tathā
asito devalo vyāsaḥ svayaṁ chaiva bravīṣhi me#BG 10.12-13:#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said

BG 10.13#अर्जुन उवाच
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान्
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम्
आहुस्त्वामृषय: सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा
असितो देवलो व्यास: स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे#arjuna uvācha
paraṁ brahma paraṁ dhāma pavitraṁ paramaṁ bhavān
puruṣhaṁ śhāśhvataṁ divyam ādi-devam ajaṁ vibhum
āhus tvām ṛiṣhayaḥ sarve devarṣhir nāradas tathā
asito devalo vyāsaḥ svayaṁ chaiva bravīṣhi me#BG 10.12-13:#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said

BG 10.14#सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव\nन हि ते भगवन्व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवा:#sarvam etad ṛitaṁ manye yan māṁ vadasi keśhava\nna hi te bhagavan vyaktiṁ vidur devā na dānavāḥ#O Krishna, I totally accept everything You have told me as the Truth. O Lord, neither gods nor the demons can understand Your true personality.#sarvam—everything
column8: keśhava—Shree Krishna, the killer of the demon named Keshi

column1: BG 10.15#स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम\nभूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते#swayam evātmanātmānaṁ vettha tvaṁ puruṣhottama\nbhūta-bhāvana bhūteśha deva-deva jagat-pate#Indeed, You alone know Yourself by Your inconceivable energy, O Supreme Personality, the Creator and Lord of all beings, the God of gods, and the Lord of the universe!#swayam—yourself

BG 10.16#वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतय:
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन्
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया#vaktum arhasyaśheṣheṇa divyā hyātma-vibhūtayaḥ
yābhir vibhūtibhir lokān imāṁs tvaṁ vyāpya tiṣhṭhasi
kathaṁ vidyām ahaṁ yogins tvāṁ sadā parichintayan
keṣhu keṣhu cha bhāveṣhu chintyo ’si bhagavan mayā#BG 10.16-17:#vaktum—to describe

BG 10.17#वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतय:
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन्
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया#vaktum arhasyaśheṣheṇa divyā hyātma-vibhūtayaḥ
yābhir vibhūtibhir lokān imāṁs tvaṁ vyāpya tiṣhṭhasi
kathaṁ vidyām ahaṁ yogins tvāṁ sadā parichintayan
keṣhu keṣhu cha bhāveṣhu chintyo ’si bhagavan mayā#BG 10.16-17:#vaktum—to describe

BG 10.18#विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन\nभूय: कथय तृप्तिर्हि शृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम्#vistareṇātmano yogaṁ vibhūtiṁ cha janārdana\nbhūyaḥ kathaya tṛiptir hi śhṛiṇvato nāsti me ’mṛitam#Tell me again in detail Your divine glories and manifestations, O Janardan. I can never tire of hearing your nectar.#vistareṇa—in detail
column6: janaārdana—Shree Krishna, he who looks after the public

column1:
BG 10.19#श्रीभगवानुवाच
हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतय:
प्राधान्यत: कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
hanta te kathayiṣhyāmi divyā hyātma-vibhūtayaḥ
prādhānyataḥ kuru-śhreṣhṭha nāstyanto vistarasya me#The Lord spoke: I shall now briefly describe My divine glories to you, O best of the Kurus, for there is no end to their detail.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Lord spoke

BG 10.20#अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थित:\nअहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च#aham ātmā guḍākeśha sarva-bhūtāśhaya-sthitaḥ\naham ādiśh cha madhyaṁ cha bhūtānām anta eva cha#O Arjun, I am seated in the heart of all living entities. I am the beginning, middle, and end of all beings.#aham—I

BG 10.21#आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्\nमरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी#ādityānām ahaṁ viṣhṇur jyotiṣhāṁ ravir anśhumān\nmarīchir marutām asmi nakṣhatrāṇām ahaṁ śhaśhī#Amongst the twelve sons of Aditi I am Vishnu
column2: amongst luminous objects I am the sun. Know Me to be Marichi amongst the maruts, and the moon amongst the stars in the night sky.#ādityānām—amongst the twelve sons of Aditi

column1: BG 10.22#वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासव:\nइन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना#vedānāṁ sāma-vedo ’smi devānām asmi vāsavaḥ\nindriyāṇāṁ manaśh chāsmi bhūtānām asmi chetanā#I am the Samaveda amongst the Vedas, and Indra amongst the celestial gods. Amongst the senses I am the mind
column2: amongst the living beings I am consciousness.#vedānām—amongst the Vedas

column1: BG 10.23#रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम्\nवसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरु: शिखरिणामहम्#rudrāṇāṁ śhaṅkaraśh chāsmi vitteśho yakṣha-rakṣhasām\nvasūnāṁ pāvakaśh chāsmi meruḥ śhikhariṇām aham#Amongst the rudras know Me to be Shankar
column2: amongst the semi-celestial beings and demons I am Kuber. I am Agni amongst the vasus and Meru amongst the mountains.#rudrāṇām—amongst the rudras
column6: vitta-īśhaḥ—the god of wealth and the treasurer of the celestial gods

column1: BG 10.24#पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम्\nसेनानीनामहं स्कन्द: सरसामस्मि सागर:#purodhasāṁ cha mukhyaṁ māṁ viddhi pārtha bṛihaspatim\nsenānīnām ahaṁ skandaḥ sarasām asmi sāgaraḥ#O Arjun, amongst priests, I am Brihaspati
column2: amongst warrior chiefs I am Kartikeya
column3: and amongst reservoirs of water, know Me to be the ocean.#purodhasām—amongst priests

column1: BG 10.25#महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम्\nयज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालय:#maharṣhīṇāṁ bhṛigur ahaṁ girām asmyekam akṣharam\nyajñānāṁ japa-yajño ’smi sthāvarāṇāṁ himālayaḥ#I am Bhrigu amongst the great seers and the transcendental Om amongst sounds. Amongst chants know Me to be the repetition of the Holy Name
column2: amongst immovable things I am the Himalayas.#mahā-ṛiṣhīṇām—among the great seers
column9: japa-yajñaḥ—sacrifice of the devotional repetition of the divine names of God

column1: BG 10.26#अश्वत्थ: सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारद:\nगन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथ: सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनि:#aśhvatthaḥ sarva-vṛikṣhāṇāṁ devarṣhīṇāṁ cha nāradaḥ\ngandharvāṇāṁ chitrarathaḥ siddhānāṁ kapilo muniḥ#Amongst trees I am the peepal tree (sacred fig tree)
column2: of the celestial sages I am Narad. Amongst the gandharvas I am Chitrath, and amongst the siddhas I am sage Kapil.#aśhvatthaḥ—the peepul tree (sacred fig tree)
column9: siddhānām—of all those who are perfected

column1: BG 10.27#उच्चै:श्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम्\nऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम्#uchchaiḥśhravasam aśhvānāṁ viddhi mām amṛitodbhavam\nairāvataṁ gajendrāṇāṁ narāṇāṁ cha narādhipam#Amongst horses know Me to be Ucchaihshrava, begotten from the churning of the ocean of nectar. I am Airavata amongst all lordly elephants, and the king amongst humans.#uchchaiḥśhravasam—Uchchaihshrava
column5: amṛita-udbhavam—begotten from the churning of the ocean of nectar

column1: BG 10.28#आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक्\nप्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्प: सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकि:#āyudhānām ahaṁ vajraṁ dhenūnām asmi kāmadhuk\nprajanaśh chāsmi kandarpaḥ sarpāṇām asmi vāsukiḥ#I am the Vajra (thunderbolt) amongst weapons and Kamadhenu amongst the cows. I am Kaamdev, the god of love, amongst all causes for procreation
column2: and amongst serpents, I am Vasuki.#āyudhānām—amongst weapons

column1: BG 10.29#अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम्\nपितृणामर्यमा चास्मि यम: संयमतामहम्#anantaśh chāsmi nāgānāṁ varuṇo yādasām aham\npitṝīṇām aryamā chāsmi yamaḥ sanyamatām aham#Amongst the snakes I am Anant
column2: amongst aquatics I am Varun. Amongst the departed ancestors I am Aryama
column3: amongst dispensers of law I am Yamraj, the lord of death.#anantaḥ—Anant
column7: varuṇaḥ—the celestial god of the ocean

column1: BG 10.30#प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां काल: कलयतामहम्\nमृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम्#prahlādaśh chāsmi daityānāṁ kālaḥ kalayatām aham\nmṛigāṇāṁ cha mṛigendro ’haṁ vainateyaśh cha pakṣhiṇām#I am Prahlad amongst the demons
column2: amongst all that controls I am time. Know me to be the lion amongst animals, and Garud amongst the birds.#prahlādaḥ—Prahlad

column1: BG 10.31#पवन: पवतामस्मि राम: शस्त्रभृतामहम्\nझषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी#pavanaḥ pavatām asmi rāmaḥ śhastra-bhṛitām aham\njhaṣhāṇāṁ makaraśh chāsmi srotasām asmi jāhnavī#Amongst purifiers, I am the wind, and amongst wielders of weapons, I am Lord Ram. Of water creatures, I am the crocodile, and of flowing rivers, I am the Ganges.#pavanaḥ—the wind

BG 10.32#सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन\nअध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वाद: प्रवदतामहम्#sargāṇām ādir antaśh cha madhyaṁ chaivāham arjuna\nadhyātma-vidyā vidyānāṁ vādaḥ pravadatām aham#O Arjun, know Me to be the beginning, middle, and end of all creation. Amongst sciences I am the science of spirituality, and in debates I am the logical conclusion.#sargāṇām—of all creations

BG 10.33#अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्व: सामासिकस्य च\nअहमेवाक्षय: कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुख:#akṣharāṇām a-kāro ’smi dvandvaḥ sāmāsikasya cha\naham evākṣhayaḥ kālo dhātāhaṁ viśhvato-mukhaḥ#I am the beginning “A” amongst all letters
column2: I am the dual word in grammatical compounds. I am the endless Time, and amongst creators I am Brahma.#akṣharāṇām—amongst all letters

column1: BG 10.34#मृत्यु: सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम्\nकीर्ति: श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृति: क्षमा#mṛityuḥ sarva-haraśh chāham udbhavaśh cha bhaviṣhyatām\nkīrtiḥ śhrīr vāk cha nārīṇāṁ smṛitir medhā dhṛitiḥ kṣhamā#I am the all-devouring death, and I am the origin of those things that are yet to be. Amongst feminine qualities I am fame, prosperity, fine speech, memory, intelligence, courage, and forgiveness.#mṛityuḥ—death
column7: bhaviṣhyatām—those things that are yet to be

column1: BG 10.35#बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम्\nमासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकर:#bṛihat-sāma tathā sāmnāṁ gāyatrī chhandasām aham\nmāsānāṁ mārga-śhīrṣho ’ham ṛitūnāṁ kusumākaraḥ#Amongst the hymns in the Samaveda know me to be the Brihatsama
column2: amongst poetic meters I am the Gayatri. Of the twelve months of the Hindu calendar I am Margsheersh, and of seasons I am spring, which brings forth flowers.#bṛihat-sāma—the Brihatsama
column4: sāmnām—amongst the hymns in the Sama Veda

column1: BG 10.36#द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्\nजयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम्#dyūtaṁ chhalayatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham\njayo ’smi vyavasāyo ’smi sattvaṁ sattvavatām aham#I am the gambling of the cheats and the splendor of the splendid. I am the victory of the victorious, the resolve of the resolute, and the virtue of the virtuous.#dyūtam—gambling

BG 10.37#वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनञ्जय:\nमुनीनामप्यहं व्यास: कवीनामुशना कवि:#vṛiṣhṇīnāṁ vāsudevo ’smi pāṇḍavānāṁ dhanañjayaḥ\nmunīnām apyahaṁ vyāsaḥ kavīnām uśhanā kaviḥ#Amongst the descendants of Vrishni, I am Krishna, and amongst the Pandavas I am Arjun. Know me to be Ved Vyas amongst the sages, and Shukracharya amongst the great thinkers.#vṛiṣhṇīnām—amongst the descendants of Vrishni

BG 10.38#दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम्\nमौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम्#daṇḍo damayatām asmi nītir asmi jigīṣhatām\nmaunaṁ chaivāsmi guhyānāṁ jñānaṁ jñānavatām aham#I am just punishment amongst means of preventing lawlessness, and proper conduct amongst those who seek victory. Amongst secrets I am silence, and in the wise I am their wisdom.#daṇḍaḥ—punishment

BG 10.39#यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन\nन तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम्#yach chāpi sarva-bhūtānāṁ bījaṁ tad aham arjuna\nna tad asti vinā yat syān mayā bhūtaṁ charācharam#I am the generating seed of all living beings, O Arjun. No creature moving or non-moving can exist without Me.#yat—which

BG 10.40#नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परन्तप\nएष तूद्देशत: प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया#nānto ’sti mama divyānāṁ vibhūtīnāṁ parantapa\neṣha tūddeśhataḥ prokto vibhūter vistaro mayā#There is no end to My divine manifestations, O conqueror of enemies. What I have declared to you is a mere sample of My infinite glories.#na—not
column7: parantapa—Arjun, the conqueror of the enemies

column1: BG 10.41#यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा\nतत्देवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम्#yad yad vibhūtimat sattvaṁ śhrīmad ūrjitam eva vā\ntat tad evāvagachchha tvaṁ mama tejo ’nśha-sambhavam#Whatever you see as beautiful, glorious, or powerful, know it to spring from but a spark of My splendor.#yat yat—whatever

BG 10.42#अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन\nविष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत्#atha vā bahunaitena kiṁ jñātena tavārjuna\nviṣhṭabhyāham idaṁ kṛitsnam ekānśhena sthito jagat#What need is there for all this detailed knowledge, O Arjun? Simply know that by one fraction of My being, I pervade and support this entire creation.#athavā—or
column5: jñātena tava—can be known by you
